articles.title: 16 bit sliced & loopable Space Hype Chiptune Tracks
articles.body: A collection of 20 tracks, each seamlessly cut to pieces so you can work with them however you want. Grab them here.

articles.title: S.E.A.M - a convenient way to quickly test & stitch your loopables
articles.body:
Introducing S.E.A.M: Segmented Evaluation & Audition Module

S.E.A.M (Segmented Evaluation & Audition Module) is a local, browser-based audio audition tool built for one job: quickly testing and “stitching” loopable, branching music systems without uploading a single file. By pointing the tool at a folder containing your WAV sample pack, the app immediately turns your on-disk layout into a playable timeline—complete with seeking, loop control, and seamless crossfades.

Low-Friction Workflow

The workflow is intentionally designed for speed. Your pack simply needs a wav/ directory, with each song represented by a subfolder. S.E.A.M infers the song’s structure directly from the WAV filenames, meaning metadata lives in your naming scheme rather than external editors:

  • Full Mixes: Use Song Name.wav.
  • Branching Transitions: Name files with an index and an allowed “next” list, such as Song Name N -> A, B, C.wav.
  • End of Chain: A simple Song Name N.wav is enough to signal the end of a segment.

Interactive Auditioning

Once loaded, S.E.A.M provides a seekable sequence of “bricks” that you can click to jump instantly, or drag to reorder and insert additional parts. Loop controls appear dynamically; if a segment is coded to transition to itself, you can toggle repeat behavior ranging from a single pass to “Forever” looping.

To ensure musical fluidity, a crossfade knob blends the tail of one song into the start of the next over a configurable overlap window—perfect for fast A/B comparisons while listening for 'seams.'

Export and Archive

When you need a tangible result, S.E.A.M can stitch your current timeline into a single preview file (WAV, MP3, or OGG) with persistent encoding settings. Per-part downloads are also available directly from the part sheet, making it easy to share specific segments.

If your day-to-day work involves loopables that must behave correctly across transitions, S.E.A.M is built to answer the question fast: “Does this transition feel right, and does this loop really hold?”
